summary: Kenneth demonstrates a covered call strategy on HIMS stock following a 30% crash, buying 100 shares at $46 and immediately selling a $48 strike call for $2.05 premium ($200/week). He explains why he avoided selling cash-secured puts during volatility and outlines decision rules for managing positions when stock price moves below entry cost.
takeaways: Sold covered call at $48 strike for $2.05 premium ($200/week target) on 100-share position
Bought shares at $46 after previous assignment at $58 (captured $200 premium + $200 stock gain = $400 total)
Avoid selling cash-secured puts during major negative catalysts (downgrade, partnership end) to prevent forced assignment at bad prices
If stock drops below covered call strike at expiration, wait for stock to recover above entry ($46) before selling next call, or sell lower strike ($44) accepting assignment loss risk
Strike selection rule: only sell call strikes above entry cost to avoid losses on assignment

strategy_candidates.change: Document the precise entry/strike/premium decision matrix for Premium Desk — codify when to sit out vs. sell lower strikes when stock crashes below entry
strategy_candidates.evidence: Kenneth explicitly walked through: bought at $46, sold $48 call for $2.05, but noted if stock stays at $42 he won't sell $48 call again and must choose between waiting for recovery or accepting loss on lower strike. This decision-making process can be templated for the $50k portfolio to automate premium capture while protecting capital.

title: Part 4: 6/23/25 $200 per WEEK on HIMS?
